howItWorks.intro: The overall gear ratio determines how many times the engine rotates for each wheel rotation, affecting acceleration, top speed, and cruising RPM.
rules: Overall ratio = transmission gear ratio × final drive ratio
RPM at speed = (speed × overall ratio × 336) ÷ tire diameter
Wheel torque = engine torque × overall ratio
Wheel force (lbs) = wheel torque ÷ (tire radius in feet)
Speed at given RPM = (RPM × tire diameter) ÷ (overall ratio × 336)
howItWorks.outro: Lower (numerically higher) gears provide more torque multiplication for acceleration. Higher (numerically lower) gears like overdrive reduce RPM for fuel economy at highway speeds.

faq.question: What is a final drive ratio?
faq.answer: The final drive ratio is the gear reduction in the differential (rear axle or transaxle). Common ratios range from 2.73 (economy) to 4.56 (performance/towing). A 3.73 axle means the driveshaft turns 3.73 times for each wheel rotation.

faq.question: How does gear ratio affect acceleration vs top speed?
faq.answer: Higher numerical ratios (like 4.10) multiply torque more, giving better acceleration but higher RPM at cruising speed. Lower ratios (like 2.73) give lower RPM and better fuel economy but slower acceleration.

faq.question: What is the 336 constant in the RPM formula?
faq.answer: The 336 constant comes from unit conversion: (miles/hour to inches/minute). Specifically, 5280 feet × 12 inches ÷ 60 minutes ÷ π ≈ 336.13. It converts linear speed to rotational speed accounting for tire circumference.

faq.question: How do I find my vehicle's gear ratios?
faq.answer: Check your owner's manual, the RPO sticker (GM vehicles), or the door jamb sticker. You can also look up your VIN online. Common axle codes are stamped on the differential cover or tag.

faq.question: Is the Gear Ratio Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Gear Ratio Calculator?
sections.body: The Gear Ratio Calculator is a free online tool designed for car owners and enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the automotive care and maintenance space. By entering your transmission gear ratio, final drive ratio, tire diameter, you get instant results including overall gear ratio, rpm at speed, wheel torque. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting overall gear ratio right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In automotive care and maintenance, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by car owners and enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to maintain your vehicle with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your transmission gear ratio and need to find the right overall gear ratio. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Transmission Gear Ratio
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your transmission gear ratio — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: final drive ratio, tire diameter, vehicle speed, engine torque. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your overall gear ratio, rpm at speed, wheel torque. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your transmission gear ratio before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both overall gear ratio and rpm at speed — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to maintain your vehicle.
If you're unsure about your engine torque, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
